specification.topic-statement: The pretty-printing helpers append consistently formatted representations of typed values to a format buffer, producing human-readable indented output for debugging and testing.

scope.in-scope: Six output operations: structure header, pointer, size_t, uint32_t, string (with character escaping), and boolean.
scope.boundary: Output is appended to a format buffer. Buffer management, allocation, and write failure handling (panic on OOM) are covered by the format buffer specification.

behavior.indentation: Every output operation begins by appending a number of space characters equal to the indent level, producing the visual nesting of fields. An indent level of zero produces no leading spaces.
behavior.structure-header:
A header line labels the start of a pretty-printed structure. It outputs the type name followed by the memory address of the structure in hexadecimal, on a single line.
Format: ` @ 0x`
behavior.pointer-field:
A pointer field displays a named pointer value. A null pointer is rendered as the literal token "NULL". A non-null pointer is rendered as its hexadecimal memory address.
Formats: - `: NULL` - `: 0x`
behavior.size-t-field:
A size_t field displays a named unsigned size value in decimal.
Format: `: `
behavior.uint32-field:
A uint32_t field displays a named 32-bit unsigned integer in decimal using a portable format specifier.
Format: `: `
behavior.string-field:
A string field displays a named string value enclosed in double quotes, with non-printable and special characters escaped. A null string pointer is rendered as the literal token "NULL" without quotes.
The string is iterated byte-by-byte for the specified length. Each byte is rendered as follows:
- `\n` → the two-character sequence `\n` - `\r` → the two-character sequence `\r` - `\t` → the two-character sequence `\t` - `\` → the two-character sequence `\\` - `"` → the two-character sequence `\"` - Control characters (0x00–0x1F) and DEL (0x7F) → `\x` followed by two hexadecimal digits - All other bytes → the literal byte character
Formats: - `: NULL` - `: ""`
Escaping operates byte-by-byte. Multi-byte UTF-8 sequences are not treated specially; each byte is examined and escaped independently.
behavior.bool-field:
A boolean field displays a named boolean value as either the literal token "true" or "false".
Formats: - `: true` - `: false`
